issue_title: Reduce logging level from critical to info.
issue_body:
Would you be open to reducing the logging level for some components of the `aiorun.run` function from `critical` to `info`? In particular, it would be great if the "default" mode of an asyncio application, in which the main function run via `aiorun.run(main())` cleanly exits via a call to `loop.stop()`, logged at the `info` level left the higher logging levels for (a) logging signal handlers and (b) logging if pending tasks are canceled.

I'd be happy to open a PR if this seems acceptable.

issue_body:
Flower seems to require the source code for the Celery worker, which if true, makes dockerization non-trivial -- since the sources will need to be in multiple places (and updated in multiple places).

Since it's just a monitor, why is this so? (i.e., can't it retrieve all available information from the broker?)

What's the best practice for using with docker?

issue_title: Correct method for parsing proprietary sentences?
issue_body:
I have a Seapath MRU.  It outputs the following proprietary sentences:
```
$PSXN,20,horiz-qual,hgt-qual,head-qual,rp-qual*csum term
$PSXN,22,gyro-calib,gyro-offs*csum term
$PSXN,23,roll,pitch,head,heave*csum term
$PSXN,24,roll-rate,pitch-rate,yaw-rate,vertical-vel*csum term
$PSXN,21,event*csum term
```
where:
```
horiz-qual: Horizontal position and velocity quality: 0 = normal, 1 = reduced performance, 2= invalid data.
hgt-qual: Height and vertical velocity quality: 0 = normal, 1 = reduced performance, 2 =invalid data.
head-qual: Heading quality: 0 = normal, 1 = reduced performance, 2 = invalid data.
rp-qual: Roll and pitch quality: 0 = normal, 1 = reduced performance, 2 = invalid data.
gyro-calib: Gyro calibration value since system start-up in degrees on format d.dd.
gyro-offs: Short-term gyro offset in degrees on format d.dd.
roll: Roll in degrees on format d.dd. Positive with port side up.
pitch: Pitch in degrees on format d.dd. Positive with bow up.
heave: Heave in metres on format d.dd. Positive down.
roll-rate: Roll rate in degrees per second on format d.dd. Positive when port side is moving upwards.
pitch-rate: Pitch rate in degrees per second on format d.dd. Positive when bow is moving upwards.
yaw-rate: Yaw rate in degrees per second on format d.dd. Positive when bow is moving towards starboard.
vertical-vel: Vertical velocity in metres per second on format d.dd. Positive when moving downwards.
event: Event code: 1 = system restart.
csum: Checksum (exclusive or) of all characters between, but not including, the preceding $ and * , hexadecimal (00 - FF).
term: CR-LF (2 bytes, values 13 and 10).
```
with sample data:
```
'$PSXN,20,0,0,0,0*3B',
'$PSXN,23,0.30,-0.97,298.57,0.13*1B',
'$PSXN,26,0,44.5000,0.7800,-0.9000,NRP*6D',
```

So I created the following class:
```
class SXN(pynmea2.ProprietarySentence):
    def __init__(self, manufacturer, data):
        super(SXN, self).__init__(manufacturer, data)
        self.sentence_type = data[1]
        if self.sentence_type == '23':
            SXN.fields = (
                ('Empty', '_'),
                ('sentence', 's'),
                ('roll', 'roll'),
                ('pitch', 'pitch'),
                ('head', 'head'),
                ('heave', 'heave')
            )
            SXN.name_to_idx = dict((f[1], i) for i, f in enumerate(SXN.fields))
```

This seems to work for the $PSXN,23 message, but as I think about supporting the others it seems kludgey. Is there a better approach?

issue_title: Example submission
issue_body:
Recursive upload:
```
import os
import yadisk


y = yadisk.YaDisk("<id-приложения>", "<secret-приложения>", "<токен>")
to_dir = '/test'
from_dir = '/home/ubuntu'
for root, dirs, files in os.walk(from_dir):
    p = root.split(from_dir)[1]
    if not y.exists(to_dir + p):
        y.mkdir(to_dir + p)
    for file in files:
        if not y.exists(to_dir + p + '/' + file):
            y.upload(from_dir + p + '/' + file, to_dir + p + '/' + file)
```

issue_title: Works on classes in different files?
issue_body:
I've looked at the answer you gave in issues #1, and your code snippet indeed works...it even works on deeper inheritance (e.g. methods in class C can get doc strings from class A).

If you put A and B in different files (called docrep_A and docrep_B), then when you do `from docrep_B import B` and `b = B()` on the command line it says: `SyntaxWarning: 'A.my_method.parameters' is not a valid key!
  @docstrings.dedent`

Do you have any thoughts on this?

issue_title: Probably incorrect Oid definition for organizationName
issue_body:
Probably there is an incorrect Oid definition for organizationName.

Name is: 'o, organizationName'
Probably should be: ['o', 'organizationName']

ldap3/protocol/oid.py, Oids.

 '2.5.4.10': ('2.5.4.10', OID_ATTRIBUTE_TYPE, 'o, organizationName', 'RFC4519'),
should be
 '2.5.4.10': ('2.5.4.10', OID_ATTRIBUTE_TYPE, ['o', 'organizationName'], 'RFC4519'),

issue_title: Operational attributes raise LDAPCursorError in abstraction layer
issue_body:
Hi Giovanni,

Thanks for your awesome library! It's super helpful and reliable. There's a bug I noticed, however, when using the abstraction layer and requesting operational attributes. ObjectDef (just as expected) is not aware of the server's operational attributes (such as **memberOf**, **entryUUID**, or **pwdChangedTime** in case of OpenLDAP). So when the search returns such attributes, it causes an error:
`ldap3.core.exceptions.LDAPCursorError: attribute 'entryUUID' not in object class 'inetOrgPerson, posixAccount, shadowAccount, inetLocalMailRecipient, ldapPublicKey' for entry uid=dummy,ou=people,dc=example,dc=org`

I'm using version 2.2.0, but the code is the same in master, in ldap3/abstract/cursor.py:83-93.
[oper.py.txt](https://github.com/cannatag/ldap3/files/822545/oper.py.txt)

issue_title: Feature request - Masking ARN
issue_body:
When recording AWS API's for unit tests. I would love to be able to mask the account number in the ARN with a fake number provided by [AWS.](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html#genref-arns) `123456789012`

It would be as simple as `re.sub(':(\d{12}:)', ':123456789012:', response_data)` somewhere here https://github.com/garnaat/placebo/blob/develop/placebo/pill.py#L242-L243. 

It would be good to have a flag if someone wanted to mask or not mask the account number of the arn. 

Thoughts?

issue_title: Not following PEP8 Whitespace in Expressions and Statements
issue_body:
autopep8 --version: 1.3.1
pycodestyle --version: 2.3.1
python --version: 3.6.1

It converts this
```
x = x*2 - 1
hypot2 = x*x + y*y
c = (a+b) * (a-b)
```
to this
```
x = x * 2 - 1
hypot2 = x * x + y * y
c = (a + b) * (a - b)
```
which is against PEP8.
[Source](http://pep8.org/#other-recommendations)

issue_title: Enhancement - check all matrices and sample y input in KalmanFilter.test_matrix_dimensions()
issue_body:
Currently it looks like test_matrix_dimensions only checks x, P, and Q.  The docs indicate it checks the size of everything, which is misleading since it doesn't check F, H, R, or a sample input.  I would prefer it to check all of those in such a way that the state of the filter is not altered.  Running down mismatched sizes is one of my least favorite things to debug and it could be streamlined.

Said slightly differently, in order to fully check the size of inputs, you need to run it, but in order to run it you need to have (mostly) correct inputs, which results in some awkward circular logic.

I think the signature could be test_matrix_dimensions(sample_input=None), where if sample_input is supplied it is additionally checked.  That would not change the previous api.

Thank you

"""this file encapsulates the bibtex handling routines.

import bibtexparser
import re
import os

def processBibtex(docname, bibtex):
    """process a raw bibtex input as downloaded from wiki.

    Main steps:
    - remove any wiki anchors
    - sanitize via bibtex library
    - write out bibtex file
    - return a list of bibtex keys, necessary for postprocessing latex

    input:
    - docname: document and subdirectory to be processed
    - bibtex: string containing the collated files

    output:
    - list of bibtex keys
    """

    # remove any wiki anchors, as defined in
    # https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Help:Anchors

    bibtex = re.sub('<( *)div(.*?)>', '', bibtex, re.S)
    bibtex = re.sub('<( *)/( *)div(.*?)>', '', bibtex, re.S)

    # sanitize via bibtex library
    parser = bibtexparser.bparser.BibTexParser()
    parser.customization = bibtexparser.customization.homogeneize_latex_encoding
    bibDB = bibtexparser.loads(bibtex,

    # lowercase all the bibtex keys, just because that's the way pandoc inserts the refs:

    writer = bibtexparser.bwriter.BibTexWriter()
    with open (os.path.join(docname, 'tex', 'bib.bib'),

    # get a list keys to return:
    keys = [x['ID'] for x in bibDB.entries]

issue_title: Decorator for multiple bots?
issue_body:
```
import asyncio
import discord
import logging
from collections import namedtuple

log = logging.getLogger('client')

entries = []

async def login(token_list, number_of_bots):
  bot_num = 0
  for e in entries:
    bot_num += 1
    token = token_list.pop(0)
    log.info("Logging into account number {} of {}".format(bot_num, number_of_bots))
    await e.client.login(token)

async def wrapped_connect(entry):
  try:
    await entry.client.connect()
  except Exception as e:
    await entry.client.close()
    log.info('We got an exception: ', e.__class__.__name__, e)
    entry.event.set()

async def check_close():
  futures = [e.event.wait() for e in entries]
  await asyncio.wait(futures)

def client(loop, token_list):
  number_of_bots = len(token_list)
  Entry = namedtuple('Entry', 'client event')
  for bot in range(number_of_bots):
    entries.append(Entry(client = discord.Client(), event = asyncio.Event()))
  loop.run_until_complete(login(token_list, number_of_bots))
  for entry in entries:
    loop.create_task(wrapped_connect(entry))
  loop.run_until_complete(check_close())
  loop.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    log.info("Bots are getting ready...")
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    token_list = [xxxxxx, xxxxx, xxxxx]
    client(loop, token_list)
```

issue_title: Servers with streaming endpoints that redirect to a different URL don't work
issue_body:
This one is tough and I don't know the best way to handle it, but...

Basically, Mastodon redirects things under /api/v1/streaming to a different URL (via 301) when configured to do so, due to the nature of various setups.

But it **only** redirects things under that endpoint, sub-endpoints like /api/v1/streaming/public return 404.

Worse still, requests strips the Authorization header when redirecting.

So, the right thing to determine the streaming endpoint:

1) Hit /api/v1/streaming to see if you get a 301 (allow_redirects=False to requests.get).
2) If you don't, you can stop here, otherwise, continue.
3) Hit the URL referenced by the Location header. This is the endpoint root.
4) All requests for the streaming server must be redirected to this URL base from now on.

I'll try to implement it, meanwhile I'm working on server-side workarounds for Mastodon.

issue_title: Unique constraint on arrays containing objects raises exception
issue_body:
We have a response for an endpoint defined as follows in our spec:
```
        responses:
          200:
            description: list of payment methods
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - data
              properties:
                data:
                  type: array
                  uniqueItems: true
                  items:
                    $ref: '#/definitions/PaymentMethod'
```
The important thing to note is that `data` here is an array containing objects. When validating a response from this endpoint, we get this exception raised:
```
 TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'
```
I'm thinking that this is because when validating uniqueness, flex uses a counter [here](https://github.com/pipermerriam/flex/blob/a8ddfae3393aea596b390022bb7263d2ca408937/flex/validation/common.py#L244), which [requires hashable types](https://docs.python.org/2/library/collections.html#collections.Counter).

Here's the full stacktrace:
```
 /app/shared/virtualenvs/venmo_platform27/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/flex/validation/response.py line 241 in validate_response
      response_validator(response, context=schema)

issue_title: Deprecate mutability of Settings objects
issue_body:
So Settings objects are currently mutable. It's even part of their recommended usage pattern. For example, from the documentation:

```pycon
>>> from hypothesis import Settings
>>> s = Settings()
>>> s.max_examples
200
>>> Settings.default.max_examples = 100
>>> t = Settings()
>>> t.max_examples
100
>>> s.max_examples
200
```

It's undocumented, but you can even assign Settings.default (historically this has not worked correctly, but it started working correctly in a recent version):

```
>>> from hypothesis import Settings
>>> Settings().max_examples
200
>>> Settings.default = Settings(max_examples=10)
>>> Settings().max_examples
10
```

I think this mostly leads to confusion, and it's particularly confusing, particularly in light of #154, in which @Bachmann1234 introduced a much better way to change settings globally.

My proposal is that all of these assignments should now start to emit deprecation warnings, with the recommendation of using the new profiles mechanism and/or local settings objects, with the goal of settings becoming immutable in Hypothesis 2.0.

Does anyone have any use cases they feel are not well represented by this? Votes aye or nay?

issue_title: Cannot get signal in MDF4 because of error in dependency_list
issue_body:
Hi everyone,

When I try to get a list of signals for a given ChannelArrayBlock, the last signal has its channel_dependencies not empty (although it is a normal Channel).
The dependency_list being not empty, it tries to get every signals like if it was a ChannelArrayBlock. This signal being itself in the dependency_list, we enter into an infinite loop and asammdf crashes when reaching Python maximal recursion limit.

When looking a bit more in detail, the dependency_list that we get for this signal, is actually the dependency_list of the ChannelArrayBlock it belongs to.

In the ChannelArrayBlock I tested, I have 3 signals, so when I put an offset of three, the get function is working again:
```
dependency_list = grp['channel_dependencies'][ch_nr + 3]
instead of:
dependency_list = grp['channel_dependencies'][ch_nr]
in get function
```

However, I am not sure yet if this offset is constant or if it depends.

I am still doing some investigation to find out why the channel_dependencies does not match with the channels we have.

issue_title: Adding a close() method
issue_body:
This a request that came up [many](https://github.com/stephan-hof/pyrocksdb/issues/10) [times](https://github.com/stephan-hof/pyrocksdb/issues/32) on the old pyrocksdb repo, but was never implemented. The reasoning seemed off to me. Without a `close()` method, there really is no way to guarantee a lock is released as Python's garbage collection can't fully be relied on for this. 

If you're consistently recreating databases from scratch (as I am in my use-case), this really complicates things. Any chance such a method can be added?

issue_title: read-only mmap results in 'file modified' events on OS X
issue_body:
I'm using watchdog in combination with a library that uses `mmap` to pull data out of a watched file. Whenever the file changes, I open it using this library, pull the data out, and close it. However, doing this results in *another* file modification event, even though the library isn't changing the file. It isn't even opening the file in write mode, and the file modification time never changes.

I'm not entirely familiar with how file watching works on OSX, so maybe this is how it should be working. It's certainly not what I expected to happen.

Here are two small Python scripts that completely reproduce the problem:

~~~~{.py}
# Usage: watcher.py [file]

import sys
import time

from watchdog.events import FileSystemEventHandler
from watchdog.observers import Observer

class MyEventHandler(FileSystemEventHandler):
    def on_modified(self, event):
        print "modified:", event.src_path

obs = Observer()
obs.schedule(MyEventHandler(), sys.argv[1], recursive=False)
obs.start()
try:
    while True:
        time.sleep(1)
except KeyboardInterrupt:
    obs.stop()
obs.join()
~~~~

~~~~{.py}
# Usage: reader.py [file]

import sys
import mmap

with open(sys.argv[1], "rb") as f:
    m = mmap.mmap(f.fileno(), 0, mmap.MAP_SHARED, mmap.PROT_READ)
    print "read byte:", repr(m[0])
    m.close()
~~~~

issue_title: The version of twilio-python currently used does not fully support Python 3.
issue_body:
**This is a follow-up of #62.**

I am currently experiencing this issue in production running on Python 3. Every request tendered to a view decorated with the twilio_view decorator is returning a 403 immediately without ever entering the view.

In that issue, a 403 forbidden was being returned consistently for views decorated with the twilio_view decorator. Later, it was discovered to be a python 2 to 3 concern. The source of the error was within twilio-python, in the RequestValidator being used by twilio_view decorator to do a simple forgery check. There is a small encoding error that will arise if using an older version of twilio-python with Python 3 (see here: https://github.com/twilio/twilio-python/issues/165).

In order to use the fixed code, the dependencies requirements for django-twilio should update the twilio-python version to accept at least version 3.6.9, where this particular fix will be included.

**EDIT: I was directed to make sure that the setup.py file matches the requirements.txt file, and found that it is out of sync. It appears that django-twilio is using the correct twilio version on installation dependencies.**

issue_title: Errors during client authorization using basic auth
issue_body:
#301 Has broken our implementation when requiring a client authorization using basic auth.

2 things which I can see have changed which have caused issues for us.

1) request.client_id is now checked before the Authorization header.
https://github.com/lepture/flask-oauthlib/pull/301/files#diff-caa6a21372b10b235c8cf7e00402330bR603
It's possible that this attribute does not exist. We were getting 'Request' object has no attribute 'client_id'.

2) Comments here https://github.com/lepture/flask-oauthlib/pull/301/files#diff-caa6a21372b10b235c8cf7e00402330bR607 indicate that the extract_params helper will replace the Authorization header with a parsed dict but it doesn't appear to be doing so and instead returning None, None for the client.
With a valid Authorization header we were getting
flask_oauthlib: DEBUG: Authenticate client None
flask_oauthlib: DEBUG: Authenticate client failed, client not found.

If we are doing something wrong, would appreciate advice on the correct way to use authenticate_client

issue_title: Odd behaviour when using numa.ledmatrix on Unicorn 8x8 HAT
issue_body:
> Description

The following code works, but get sporadic behaviour - most commonly missed pixels:

```
import time

from luma.led_matrix.device import neopixel, UNICORN_HAT
from luma.core.render import canvas

device = neopixel(width=8, height=8, mapping=UNICORN_HAT)

for y in range(device.height):
    for x in range(device.width):
        with canvas(device) as draw:
            draw.point((x, y), fill="green")
        time.sleep(0.5)
```

> Any observations and patterns

On launch, the entire HAT will light up briefly.  Sometimes it's white/bright colours, sometimes it's a random selection of colours.  Green dot begins moving across the display, but after a short while - usually by line #3 or #4 it will start 'jumping' (missing pixels).

> Your device type (PiZero / 2B / 3B, etc)

Pi 3B (less than a week old), no mods other than the HAT itself.

> OS and kernel version (paste output of uname -a)

`Linux ninja 4.4.38-v7+ #938 SMP Thu Dec 15 15:22:21 GMT 2016 armv7l GNU/Linux`

`$ cat /etc/*-release`

```
PRETTY_NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux 8 (jessie)"
NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux"
VERSION_ID="8"
VERSION="8 (jessie)"
ID=raspbian
ID_LIKE=debian

SUPPORT_URL="http://www.raspbian.org/RaspbianForums"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://www.raspbian.org/RaspbianBugs"
```

No mods other than enabled SPI, disabled boot to Desktop Manager and enabled SSH

> Output of dmesg after running the demos

Do you want entire output?  Ok to paste in comments?

> What amp rating does the power supply provide?

2.5A (https://www.amazon.co.uk/gp/product/B01M03T0UB/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

> What other peripherals are connected? screen, keyboard, mouse, WIFI, etc?

None - just power and the HAT.  Wifi is built-in on Pi 3.  Pi is accessed via SSH.

> Do you get the same erratic behaviour if you use the official Pimoroni drivers?

No, these work consistently fine.

> On the 8x4 Unicorn pHAT, I had to solder the header on - is this the same for the 8x8 HAT? - if so check for any bad solder joints

issue_title: Support multiple values for a given parameter
issue_body:
Pyramid has excellent support for URIs like /foo?x=bar&x=baz in the form of a [Multidict](http://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/pyramid/en/1.0-branch/narr/webob.html#multidict). We want to support this option within pyramid_swagger so a URL like:

`/foo?x=bar&x=baz`

can **successfully** validate against a schema like:

```
{
   paramType: "query",
   type: "string",
   paramType: "query",
}
```

This requires query argument validation supporting both the primitive value and a MultiDict of the primitive, as long as all values satisfy the primitive type.

issue_title: This code is causing failures for JSON responses
issue_body:
https://github.com/striglia/pyramid_swagger/blob/e89cf43fc91d382d9255669e0cd9f12543988c8b/pyramid_swagger/tween.py#L545-L549

Caused this ticket:

https://github.com/Pylons/pyramid/pull/2971

In WebOb 1.7 it was changed so that those content-types that don't have a charset parameter (mostly application/*) would no longer send an charset content-type parameter.

JSON is specified as always being either UTF-8 or UTF-16/32 with BOM. There is no charset parameter, and clients are explicitly told to ignore it. To be a good internet citizen you should be strict in what you send... and WebOb 1.7 really tried to make that a reality.

WebOb 1.7 introduced `Response.default_body_encoding` so that `Response.text` may be used even in cases where there is no charset defined for the content-type, although I wouldn't necessarily encourage this use, instead I would recommend setting response.body with a binary type string instead after properly encoding the data.

Thanks!

issue_title: Setting to disable GZIP compression?
issue_body:
Amazon CloudFront recently added (opt-in) support for auto-magic transparent GZIP compression.

This means it is no longer needed to pre-compress the S3 objects for gzipped serving over HTTP if you use CloudFront and opted in to this. 

So it would be cool if we'd have a setting to disable the GZIP compression in django-s3-storage (maybe with a use-case note in the readme in case people missed [the CF announcement](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/new-gzip-compression-support-for-amazon-cloudfront/)).

issue_title: Reimplement based on naturally ordered sets?
issue_body:
The [new Pypy dict](http://morepypy.blogspot.be/2015/01/faster-more-memory-efficient-and-more.html) features natural ordering (by insertion order), faster iteration and reduced memory consumption.

It's based on [a design/concept by Raymond Hettinger on python-dev](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/python-dev/2012-December/123028.html) which he intended in big part [to use for sets](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/python-dev/2012-December/123207.html). Applying the design to a set avoids storing a bunch of extra doubly-linked-list pointers to provide iteration ordering.

issue_title: Supporting a wider range of actions types
issue_body:
I've been improving [Django Admin Row Actions](https://github.com/DjangoAdminHackers/django-admin-row-actions) recently and I wanted to reuse the row actions as object actions.

Row Actions supports a few things that Object actions doesn't:
1. Passing in just a link
2. Methods on the model as well as the modeladmin
3. Passing in lambdas or wrapped methods such as partials (useful for dynamically generating actions

Would you be interested in any of the above for Object Actions? I'll clean up the code a touch more if so.

issue_title: The new release to PyPI is needed!
Handling of generic procedure interfaces
issue_body:
When declaring a generic procedure interface, the generic interface having the same name as one of the contained procedures can trigger a `Variable declared twice in scope` error from `fortls`. I thought this may just be incorrect fortran (it definitely seems bad and confusing) but `gfortran -c test.f03` compiles without errors and doesn't complain even with `-Wall` specified (`crayftn` also has no issues), so I think it must be acceptable. Not a massive issue as it doesn't cause parsing to fail, but detecting this specific case and demoting to a warning might be useful for agreement with the compiler.

```fortran
module m_test

interface test_proc
    module procedure test_proc
end interface test_proc

contains

subroutine test_proc( )
end subroutine test_proc

end module m_test
```

issue_title: Add support for COUNT option in SCAN and its families
issue_body:
Redis' default is to fetch at most 10 items per each SCAN commands, but asyncio_redis currently does not provide any means to modify this number, which may be required for performance tuning.
Redis' documentation says that it is okay to change the count number during cursor iteration, so it would be good to add an optional argument `count` to `fetchone()` and `fetchall()` methods in the Cursor class and pass it to the `_scanfunc()` via `_fetch_more()`.

issue_title: CSRF attack possibility?
issue_body:
Is a REST API around apscheduler not coming with the risk of CSRF attacks, considering that someone could add a job by having you click on a link which sends a HTTP POST request to add a job? https://github.com/viniciuschiele/flask-apscheduler/blob/master/flask_apscheduler/views.py#L35-L46 ?
I'd allow access to flask-apscheduler only from localhost, but it might not be sufficient (see: https://lwn.net/Articles/703485/), if you just click a bad link, which knows about your flask-apscheduler service and adds a job to it. If you don't run a webbrowser on your production server you're fine, because then you don't click on any links, but maybe it should still be considered?

A workaround could be to add jobs via a configuration file (so you are secured by linux file permissions) and then send a REST API request to reload jobs from the configuration file?

Maybe also adding authentication to HTTP requests could improve security?

==== 2D code ====
import mxnet as mx
from mxnet import gluon as g
import numpy as np

ctx = mx.cpu()
img = np.zeros((1, 3, 7))
img = img.reshape(img.shape[0], 3, 7, 1)
dataLoader = g.data.DataLoader(img, batch_size=1)

net = g.nn.Sequential()
with net.name_scope():
    net.add(g.nn.Conv2DTranspose(channels=3, kernel_size=(3,1), activation='relu'))

net.collect_params().initialize(mx.init.Normal(sigma=0.05))

for data in dataLoader:

issue_title: Cannot create AdVideo via AdAccount
issue_body:
FB supports 2 different ways of creating ad videos:

https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/reference/ad-account/advideos

and 

https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/advideo/v2.9

However, the parameters to create an ad video is different between the 2 calls - 

the second approach, to create AdVideo while setting the parent_id to the ad account accepts filepath, while the other method of creating AdVideo as part of the an AdAccount accepts file_url.

FB SDK deprecated the second option (of creating a directly AdVideo), however, I don't find an equivalent method in AdAccount -> create ad video that allows uploading from file path.

See AdVideo:

https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/adobjects/advideo.py

See the way to create it via AdAccount:
https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/adobjects/adaccount.py

issue_title: Behaviour of HasTraits.observe(<handler>, <trait>, type=All)
issue_body:
I tested the dynamic observe on HasTraits attributes with the following example:

```python
from traitlets import HasTraits, Float, All

class TestTraitlets(HasTraits):
    a = Float()

    def __init__(self):
        HasTraits.__init__(self)
        self.observe(self._a_notified, "a", type=All)

    def _a_notified(self, d):
        print("'{name}' Changed: {old} to {new}".format(**d))

t = TestTraitlets()
print("Initial value of a: ", t.a)
print("Changing a to 2:")
t.a = 2
print("reassigning a to 2:")
t.a = 2
print("Changing a to 3:")
t.a = 3
```
When running it provides the following output:
```
Initial value of a:  0.0
Changing a to 2:

issue_title: Generic database target
issue_body:
In some of our pipelines, we have long running tasks which don't have clear output. For example, a task which reads in data from multiple tables in one schema, and updates (rather than inserts) into a single table in another schema. As for as I can tell, this isn't the use case for something like the `sqla.CopyToTable` task. 

My workaround was a slightly more generic version of the `mysqldb.MySqlTarget` where we omit the update table column and simply keep a record of every successful task run, using `task_id` as the update ID. 
1. Is there a better pattern for cases like this that I have missed? 
2. If not, I'd be happy to submit a PR after cleaning it up a bit.

issue_title: Sqlalchemy issue when setting multiple relationships in the same call
issue_body:
I've been running into some issues over the last few months setting multiple relationships in the same API call.  Here's an example error message I received earlier today trying to set two relationships in the same call: 

`sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError: (raised as a result of Query-invoked autoflush; consider using a session.no_autoflush block if this flush is occurring prematurely) (psycopg2.IntegrityError) null value in column "conversation_id" violates not-null constraint`

The problem seems to be related to the `apply_relationships` method in the `SqlalchemyDataLayer`.  As it loops through the relationship fields it tries to fetch the `related_object` and immediately sets the relationship with `setattr(obj, key, related_obj)`.  Although this works for the first relationship, it fails with the above error when it tries to fetch the second `related_object`.

I am able to get around this problem with the following custom data layer that waits to set the relationships until all of them have been fetched.  I'm not sure if this is the "right" way to solve it, but I wanted to post it here in case anyone else finds it helpful or can shed light on a better way.

```
class ImprovedDataLayer(SqlalchemyDataLayer):

    def apply_relationships(self, data, obj):
        """Apply relationship provided by data to obj
        :param dict data: data provided by the client
        :param DeclarativeMeta obj: the sqlalchemy object to plug relationships to
        :return boolean: True if relationship have changed else False
        """
        relationships_to_apply = []
        relationship_fields = get_relationships(self.resource.schema)
        for key, value in data.items():
            if key in relationship_fields:
                related_model = getattr(obj.__class__, key).property.mapper.class_
                related_id_field = self.resource.schema._declared_fields[
                    relationship_fields[key]].id_field

                if isinstance(value, list):
                    related_objects = []

                    for identifier in value:
                        related_object = self.get_related_object(related_model,
                                                                 related_id_field,
                                                                 {'id': identifier})
                        related_objects.append(related_object)

                    # setattr(obj, key, related_objects)
                    relationships_to_apply.append({
                        'field': key,
                        'value': related_objects
                    })
                else:
                    related_object = None

                    if value is not None:
                        related_object = self.get_related_object(related_model,
                                                                 related_id_field,
                                                                 {'id': value})

                    # setattr(obj, key, related_object)
                    relationships_to_apply.append({
                        'field': key,
                        'value': related_object
                    })

        # Wait to set relationships until all have been fetched from the db
        for relationship in relationships_to_apply:
            setattr(obj, relationship['field'], relationship['value'])
```

issue_title: Timezone offset from UTC is wrong when not in DST
issue_body:
It turns out that the `Europe/Paris` timezone just got **out** of DST and is now in UTC+1 back from UTC+2. It means that dates should currently be recorded with +0100. `date` does it correctly:

    $ TZ='Europe/Paris' date +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"
    2015-11-04T08:45:34+0100

However, GitPython uses `time.altzone` to guess dates in commits, which yields +0200 instead of +0100:

    $ TZ='Europe/Paris' python -c 'import time; print(-time.altzone/3600)'
    2.0

While the date are still correct, the timezone is now wrong. Indeed, using `time.altzone` is only correct when:

 1. `time.daylight` is non zero, eg. the current timezone has a DST defined at some point in the year (always true for Europe/Paris, but always false for Indian/Reunion)
 2. DST is currently active, which you can check with `time.localtime().tm_isdst > 0`.

To fix this issue, you should [use this code to get the UTC offset in seconds](http://stackoverflow.com/a/3168394/481584).

    is_dst = time.daylight and time.localtime().tm_isdst > 0
    utc_offset = time.altzone if is_dst else time.timezone

You still need to multiply by -1 and divide by 3600, but you will get the correct offset this way.

Thank you!

If it's possible, could you include also element's location in the error message?

issue_title: Starting server on background
issue_body:
The init currently calls serve_forever() that prevents execution from continuing.
Init also registers signal handlers - this is only allowed in the main thread.
Thus currently it is not possible to start the server on background and continuing working on something else on the main thread.

How do I know it takes `repoze.zcml==0.4`? Well in the traceback below you see this:

issue_title: "pipenv install --user"
issue_body:
Hello

Can we imagine a "--user" option for "pipenv install", that would allow a content of a Pipfile/Lock to be installed into the ~/.local directory of the user? Pip provides this option, and is used by pipenv to update itself.
I should not be the "normal" way of using pipenv, but since there is a "--system" that install on the system (requiring sudo permission), a similar "--user" can be useful.

I am looking for this option especially for embedded the application inside a docker image, most of a time it is fine to recreate a virtualenv inside the docker (it is still better than using "--system" even from inside a docker), but sometimes we want the application directly callable by the docker user.

thanks

issue_title: scandir as dependency: UnicodeDecodeError
issue_body:
If I set `scandir` as a dependency, then installation on a system that does not already have scandir fails with `UnicodeDecodeError`.

## Versions
- OS `Ubuntu 16.04`
- Python `2.7.12`
- setuptools `34.1.0`
- pip `9.0.1`

## Steps to reproduce

Start with a clean environment that does not already have scandir installed. I'm using the Dockerimage `ubuntu:16.04`, with the following system configuration:

```
apt-get update && apt-get install python-pip -y
pip install -U pip setuptools
```

Create a test project and a `setup.py` file:

```
from setuptools import setup

setup(
    name = "failr",
    version = "0.0.1",
    install_requires = [

)

## Expected outcome
Package is installed.

## Actual outcome
Installation fails with `UnicodeDecodeError` (see log above).

## Useful information
If I re-run the setup.py install command above a second time, it works.

If I install scandir with pip (`pip install scandir`) before running the install command above, it works fine on the first attempt.

The character referenced in the error comes from the file name `unicodƏ.txt` (also seen in the log). When the `join()` function in `posixpath.py` tries to treat it as ASCII to convert to unicode it fails, because `Ə` is not an ASCII character.

issue_title: All arguments for all functions should have native types
Group filters into sub groups
issue_body:
Most of filter rules are domain specific. If we put filters start with "|", "||", "@@||" into their own list of rules with a given domain, then the giant regex will be cut into 1/3 of the original size. The domain specified rules will be quite short.

Each domain can then be put into a dict mapping to the respected filter rules.

And most rest of the rules have the string "ad" in it. if we seperate rest of the rules by whether a rule has "ad" in it, we can have a much smaller regex for urls without "ad".

```
domain_rules = {
"host1": merged_regex,
"host2": merged_regex
...
}
rules_with_ad = merged_regex
rules_without_ad = merged_regex
```
Since most false postive urls don't match domains and has no `ad` in them, matching false positive should be much faster.

issue_title: `Site` does not exist during migrations for `sharedcontent` plugin.
issue_body:
During migrations, Django tries to evaluate the default value for the `site` field in order to create the model.

The default is a callable that returns `Site.objects.get_current().pk`. On a fresh database, no `Site` object exists.

If `django-fluent-pages` is also installed, its migrations will actually create a default `Site` object if none exist. But when `django-fluent-contents` is used on its own, this doesn't happen.

Perhaps the `make_site` code from `django-fluent-pages` should be abstracted into `django-fluent-utils` and called by `fluent_contents.plugins.sharedcontent.utils.get_current_site_id()`?

See: https://github.com/edoburu/django-fluent-pages/blob/master/fluent_pages/migrations/0001_initial.py#L10

issue_title: Return instrumental magnitude along with aperture sum
issue_body:
The [aperture_photometry](https://photutils.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api/photutils.aperture_photometry.html) function currently returns just the sum of the flux within the aperture. Since the [instrumental magnitude](http://stsdas.stsci.edu/cgi-bin/gethelp.cgi?phot) can be obtained with:

    mag = -2.5 * log10((aperture_sum - bckg) / itime)

where `bckg` is the sky value estimate and `itime` is the exposure time, simply requesting the user to input the exposure would be enough to return the instrumental magnitude.

This would seem to be a much more useful quantity than the aperture sum?

issue_title: anomaly_likelihood_test: Has Broken Test (Fix is in comment)
issue_body:
## The text below contains both the description of the problem, and the fix to make the test work, and the code improvement of the class being tested.
 
**.../nupic/tests/unit/nupic/algorithms/anomaly_likelihood_test.py line#226**
testEstimateAnomalyLikelihoodsMalformedRecords() - ***actually tests nothing.***

#### the lines:

data1 = _generateSampleData(mean=0.2)
data1 = data1 + [(2, 2), (2, 2, 2, 2), (), (2)]  # Malformed records
    
likelihoods, avgRecordList, estimatorParams = (
      an.estimateAnomalyLikelihoods(data1[0:1000])
)

#### should be changed to:

data1 = _generateSampleData(mean=0.2)
data1 = data1**[0:996]** + [(2, 2), (2, 2, 2, 2), (), (2)]  # Malformed records
    
likelihoods, avgRecordList, estimatorParams = (
      an.estimateAnomalyLikelihoods(data1[0:1000])
)

... so that when estimateAnomalyLikelihoods() is called with **data1[0:1000]** , the "malformed" records will be included.

##### A corresponding change is needed in anomaly_likelihood.py in order to have the 2nd to last record not cause an exception (i.e. the "()" in  [(2, 2), (2, 2, 2, 2), (), (2)]). 

_This is so because the test method as previously written never actually passed the above malformed records in to the method being tested; now that they are being passed in, the code which "detects" bad records is now being executed._

**The required change is on line#486 in the _anomalyScoreMovingAverage() method:**

#### Instead of:
if len(record) != 3:
   if verbosity >= 0:
      print "Malformed record:", record
      continue

#### Should be:
if **not isinstance(record, list) or** len(record) != 3:
   if verbosity >= 0:
      print "Malformed record:", record
      continue

issue_title: Allow CrossRef search in BibEdit with FREE "Query Services account"
issue_labels: [{'id': 96301915, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Branch:%20legacy', 'name': 'Branch: legacy', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': False}, {'id': 96241276, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Component:%20BibEdit', 'name': 'Component: BibEdit', 'color': 'ededed', 'default': False}, {'id': 96238448, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'Type: enhancement', 'color': 'ff5500', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-01-31

The newly introduced CrossRef utils allow registered CrossRef users to search -and import- records into Invenio by just entering the DOI. This was a highly anticipated addon!

For end users to be able to use it, Invenio admin must specify a CrossRef username/password in invenio(-local).conf Unfortunately, such an account is a privilege of CrossRef members only, a service that comes with a premium (let alone all the paperwork that needs to be filled).

Luckily, there is an alternative to becoming a full member and still be able to search CrossRef via OpenURL, using a FREE Query Services account! The only 'catch' is that this account allows up to a ~10^5 searches/year (more than enough for most of us...)

One must simply register (and verify) an email here: http://www.crossref.org/requestaccount/
that will be used as the 'username' for the searches. NO password is required!

The syntax for searching CrossRef with a free "Query Services account" is slightly different and described here: http://help.crossref.org/#using_the_open_url_resolver

So, Invenio could easily take advantage of this no-paperwork/free service from CrossRef and feed data into BibEdit with just the following trivial crossrefutils.py patch:

```
--- crossrefutils.py    2013-01-31 19:22:08.278990797 +0200
+++ crossrefutils.py.orig       2013-01-31 19:14:36.222417135 +0200
@@ -52,10 +52,8 @@
     doi = doi.strip()

     # Getting the data from external source
-    url = "http://www.crossref.org/openurl/?pid=" +  CFG_CROSSREF_USERNAME
-    if CFG_CROSSREF_PASSWORD:
-       url += ":" + CFG_CROSSREF_PASSWORD
-    url += "&noredirect=tru&id=doi:" + doi
+    url = "http://www.crossref.org/openurl/?pid=" +  CFG_CROSSREF_USERNAME \
+        + ":" + CFG_CROSSREF_PASSWORD + "&noredirect=tru&id=doi:" + doi
     request = urllib2.Request(url)
     response = urllib2.urlopen(request)
     header = response.info().getheader('Content-Type')
```

invenio.conf could also get an updated explanatory text like:

```
## CFG_CROSSREF_USERNAME -- the username used when sending request
## to the Crossref site.
## In case a Query Services account is used, enter the registered email instead.
CFG_CROSSREF_USERNAME =

## CFG_CROSSREF_PASSWORD -- the password used when sending request
## to the Crossref site.
## In case a Query Services account is used, please leave this field empty.
CFG_CROSSREF_PASSWORD =
```

issue_title: Would it be possbile to include the .dlls in the binary download?
issue_body:
Hi,

would it be possible to include the .dll files in the binary downloads for Windows?
The .dlls are kind of needed to make the JNI wrappers work. This is especially a problem for the encoder as there is no Java implementation (which is a huge adoption problem IMHO).

I already tried to build the .dlls myself with CMake and MinGW, but I was not able to make the JNI encoder wrapper work with them. I always got an UnsatisfiedLinkError on instantiation with libbrotlicommon.dll and libbrotlienc.dll loaded.

>java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: org.brotli.wrapper.enc.EncoderJNI.nativeCreate([J)Ljava/nio/ByteBuffer;
        at org.brotli.wrapper.enc.EncoderJNI.nativeCreate(Native Method)
        at org.brotli.wrapper.enc.EncoderJNI.access$000(EncoderJNI.java:15)
        at org.brotli.wrapper.enc.EncoderJNI$Wrapper.<init>(EncoderJNI.java:36)
        at org.brotli.wrapper.enc.Encoder.compress(Encoder.java:159)
        at org.brotli.wrapper.enc.Encoder.compress(Encoder.java:196)

I suspect it has to do with [gcc using symbols incompatible with JNI](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/10274278/jni-unsatisfiedlinkerror-on-native-method) and me not able to make CMake to do the right thing.

Anyways, it would be nice if we could have the Windows libs along with the executable similar to the other platforms.

Don't name the module "cmd"

I created a CLI module for my weekend project and called it "cmd.py".
This leads to the following exception:
```
/home/marcel/.virtualenvs/ultrabak/bin/python /home/marcel/git/ultrabak/ultrabak/cmd.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/marcel/git/ultrabak/ultrabak/cmd.py", line 1, in <module>
    import fire
  File "/home/marcel/git/python-fire/fire/__init__.py", line 21, in <module>
    from fire.core import Fire
  File "/home/marcel/git/python-fire/fire/core.py", line 62, in <module>
    from fire import completion
  File "/home/marcel/git/python-fire/fire/completion.py", line 25, in <module>
    from fire import inspectutils
  File "/home/marcel/git/python-fire/fire/inspectutils.py", line 23, in <module>
    import IPython
  File "/home/marcel/.virtualenvs/ultrabak/lib/python3.5/site-packages/ipython-5.3.0-py3.5.egg/IPython/__init__.py", line 48, in <module>
    from .core.application import Application
  File "/home/marcel/.virtualenvs/ultrabak/lib/python3.5/site-packages/ipython-5.3.0-py3.5.egg/IPython/core/application.py", line 25, in <module>
    from IPython.core import release, crashhandler
  File "/home/marcel/.virtualenvs/ultrabak/lib/python3.5/site-packages/ipython-5.3.0-py3.5.egg/IPython/core/crashhandler.py", line 28, in <module>
    from IPython.core import ultratb
  File "/home/marcel/.virtualenvs/ultrabak/lib/python3.5/site-packages/ipython-5.3.0-py3.5.egg/IPython/core/ultratb.py", line 119, in <module>
    from IPython.core import debugger
  File "/home/marcel/.virtualenvs/ultrabak/lib/python3.5/site-packages/ipython-5.3.0-py3.5.egg/IPython/core/debugger.py", line 46, in <module>
    from pdb import Pdb as OldPdb
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/pdb.py", line 135, in <module>
    class Pdb(bdb.Bdb, cmd.Cmd):
AttributeError: module 'cmd' has no attribute 'Cmd'
```

After renaming it to "cli.py" everything works as expected. The reason is that the cmd module is added to the pythonpath if it is executed like this and covers python's cmd module. It works if the cmd module is executed as a submodule (python -m ultrabak.cmd or using entry_points).

As cmd.py could be a common name for a CLI, I'd suggest to add a note somewhere in the docs.

issue_title: Reason is missing
issue_body:
The field [reason](https://docs.python.org/3/library/http.client.html#http.client.HTTPResponse.reason) is missing from the mocked request. It is usually set to HTTP status code's readable name (eg. "OK" "Not Found")

This make some libraries, like [requests_toolbelt dumping](https://toolbelt.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dumputils.html) unhappy.

issue_title: SanitizePasswordsProcessor and local vars
issue_body:
I'm experiencing an issue where values in certain locations are being masked by the `SanitizePasswordsProcessor` and not in others, namely in local variables pulled from stack traces.

The default regex for the processor is `^(?:\d[ -]*?){13,16}$` and when I have code such as this in my app:
```
@app.route('/')
def index():
    current_app.sentry.client.context.merge({'extra': {
        'example': '4242424242424242'}})
    example_var = "4242424242424242"
    raise Exception('foo')
```

the example from the extra context is masked correctly with asterisks, and the example_var from the stack trace is in plain text with quotes around it. It appears when it enters the sanitize method it is a string wrapped in single quotes which fails `SanitizePasswordsProcessor.VALUES_RE`.

Version information:
```
Python 3.6.5
raven==6.8.0
```

Is this expected behavior?

issue_title: Iterating over resource.Resource.seq() throws TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
issue_body:
Hi,

experimenting with the rdflib.resource.Resource class, I came upon what I think is an issue with Resource.seq(). This call returns an object of generator type, but iterating over that object gives an exception in some cases. It seems that a call to Resource.seq() can result in None, rather than a in generator that does not yield anything, which is what I would expect.

Is there an issue here, or do I misunderstand?

Example code follows (python 3.5, rdflib 4.2.1):

```python
from rdflib import Graph

g = Graph()

g.parse(data="""
<rdf:RDF
  xmlns:rdf='http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#'
  xmlns:vCard='http://www.w3.org/2001/vcard-rdf/3.0#'
   >

  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://somewhere/JohnSmith/">
    <vCard:FN>John Smith</vCard:FN>
    <vCard:N rdf:parseType="Resource">
    <vCard:Family>Smith</vCard:Family>
    <vCard:Given>John</vCard:Given>
    </vCard:N>
  </rdf:Description>

  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://somewhere/RebeccaSmith/">
    <vCard:FN>Becky Smith</vCard:FN>
    <vCard:N rdf:parseType="Resource">
    <vCard:Family>Smith</vCard:Family>
    <vCard:Given>Rebecca</vCard:Given>
    </vCard:N>
  </rdf:Description>

  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://somewhere/SarahJones/">
    <vCard:FN>Sarah Jones</vCard:FN>
    <vCard:N rdf:parseType="Resource">
    <vCard:Family>Jones</vCard:Family>
    <vCard:Given>Sarah</vCard:Given>
    </vCard:N>
  </rdf:Description>

  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://somewhere/MattJones/">
    <vCard:FN>Matt Jones</vCard:FN>
    <vCard:N
    vCard:Family="Jones"
    vCard:Given="Matthew"/>
  </rdf:Description>

</rdf:RDF>
""")

r = g.resource('http://somewhere/MattJones/')

print(r.identifier)
# prints "http://somewhere/MattJones/"

s = r.seq()

print(type(s))
# prints "<class 'generator'>"

for i in s:
    print(i)
# gives "TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable"
```

issue_title: Support 'overall ratio' metrics (e.g. conversion rate/return rate) as opposed to per-entity ratios
issue_body:
In case of conversion rate or return rate, the KPI can be either defined on the entity level or aggregated over all entities, and we probably want to support both of them.

After some discussion, we came up with the idea to reweight the data of the individual entities to calculate the overall ratio statistics. This enables to use the existing statistics.delta() function to calculate the overall ratio statistics (using normal assumtion or bootstraping).

Calculating return rates
--------------------------------------------
As an example let's look at return rates which are typically calculated (on individual entity level) as:
$individual_rr=1/n \sum_{i=1}^n \frac{ARTICLES-RETURNED_i}{ARTICLES-ORDERED_i}$

The overall ratio is a reweighting of individual_rr to reflect not the entities' contributions (e.g. contribution per custormer) but overall equally contributions to the return return rate  (i.e. return rate on overall article basis) which can be formulated as:
$$overall_rr= \frac{1/n \sum_{i=1}^n ARTICLES-RETURNED_i}{1/n \sum_{i=1}^n ARTICLES-ORDERED_i}$$

Overall as reweighted Individual
--------------------------------------------
One can calculate the overall_rr from the individual_rr using the following reweighting  (easily proved by paper and pencile):
$$overall_rr=1/n \sum_{i=1}^n \alpha_i \frac{ARTICLES-RETURNED_i}{ARTICLES-ORDERED_i}$$
with 
$$alpha_i= n \frac{ARTICLES-ORDERED_i}{\sum_{i=1}^n ARTICLES-ORDERED_i}

Weighted delta function
--------------------------------
To have such functionality as a more generic approach in ExpAn we can use introduce a "weighted delta" function in statistics. Its input are 
- The entity based variables (such as \frac{ARTICLES-RETURNED}{ARTICLES-ORDERED}) - for treatment and control 
- A variable that specifies the quanities per entitiy (such as ARTICLES-ORDERED) - for treatment and control

With this input it calculates alpha as described above and outputs the result of statistics.delta()

Be able to define something like:

  Labels:

    description: |

issue_title: Add support for Swagger tags
issue_body:
The Swagger spec provides support for assigning one or more 'tags' for each method. This is used by tools like Swagger UI to group methods, often by tags representing each resource. This results in very nice groupings as you can see in the PetStore example for pet, store, and user [here](http://petstore.swagger.io).

Implementation suggestions:

- Add an argument to the route decorator: tag=dict(name='pet', description='Everything about your pets')
- The spec lets you have multiple tags, but I think starting with a single tag might be okay and it looks simpler.
- When generating the swagger output, insert the tags= JSON with the value from the route view for name.
- Also append a tags_list entry every time you see a new tag name= value with both the name and description.
- Once all route JSON has been generated, output a tags=[...] key with the tags_list we've built.

Note that Swagger JSON for a route tag (under method) looks like:

```JSON
"tags": [
    "pet"
]
```

And the top-level tags key:

```JSON
"tags": [
    {
        "name": "pet",
        "description": "Everything about your pets"
    }
```

issue_title: Test for a low ratio of direct metabolites in the biomass reaction
issue_body:
#### Problem description

> Some biomass precursors are taken from the media and directly consumed by the biomass reaction. It might not be a problem for some ions or metabolites with known auxotrophy, but when there are too many of these metabolites, it points to artefacts of gap-filling. This mostly happens in case of automatically-generated models. Starting with a complicated automatically generated biomass formulation followed by gap-filling (which basically tries to minimise the added reactions), would lead to many biomass metabolites being "direct mets". It might be useful to report the ratio of biomass metabolites that are direct mets.

- Parizad Babayi, comment on the manuscript.

issue_title: [Request] Verify User Project methods comments are not ambiguous
issue_body:
General Request:

Verify that the comments are clear for methods which accept User Project with and without Requester Pays.

Here's a distinction between Requester Pays and User Project:
User Project: Is a request metadata field that is used to assign Storage operational costs to a specified project.

Requester Pays: Is a bucket-level metadata field that is used to enforce the use of the User Project metadata field to assign operational costs when an operation is made on a Bucket and its objects.

issue_title: How's it possible to route for mobile applications that doesn't support socks5 protocol?
issue_body: Shadowsocks won't work for *Google Play* and other mobile phone applications like *Instagram* or *Tumblr*, I think the problem is these applications doesn't support socks5 tunnel. issue_title: API Gateway request integration does not support for VPC_PROXY integration.
issue_body:
HI,
In API Gateway it supports to have VPC Link integration with "Use Proxy Integration" is set to True. So it takes the type as VPC_PROXY.

CloudFormation they have "connectionType" to handle this VPC_LINK.  https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/api-reference/resource/integration/#type

But it seems this property is missing in the troposphere APIGateway Integration class (https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/master/troposphere/apigateway.py).

Please correct me if I am wrong here.

So would like to know is there any time frame that you will include this feature ?

Thanks in advance.
Nirosh

issue_title: ./pants dependencies --dependencies-transitive doesn't actually walk the transitive deps
issue_body:
Quoting @kwlzn in issue #3830 

> also afaik, ./pants dependencies --dependencies-transitive doesn't actually walk the transitive deps of python libraries - just jvm deps. that one might be worth another github issue.

I think it would be nice to be able to show all the transitive dependencies brought in by other python libraries with `python_requirement_library(..)`, as well as annotating which library required that other library. So something like 

```
retrying >= 1.1 (resolved to retrying == 1.1)
functools >= 3.2 (required from retrying == 1.1, resolved to functools == 3.2)
```

Or something like that would be ideal. That way it would be easy to debug why you might have ambiguous resolvables, like oh functools wants setuptools >= 2.1, and retrying wants setuptools <= 2.0 or something.

Thanks!

issue_title: Python legacy resources are not included in `filedeps`
issue_body:
Because the resources captured by `resources=globs` (as opposed to `resource_targets=[':address']`) on a Python target are added to a synthetic target, they do not show up in filedeps as owned by the python target.

Example:
```
./pants filedeps testprojects/src/python/sources
```

(this was discovered while implementing #3506, and should be addressed by #3560)

issue_title: Pants python thrift building allows no control over the default thrift requirement.
issue_body:
Due to the way the pex resolver works today (like pip, in breadth 1st rounds), the unconstrained thrift dependency pants' PythonChroot (and ThriftBuilder) inject in the 1st resolution round can easily conflict with thrift requirements in later (deeper transitive dep) rounds.  The solution for the pex cli or in pip itself is to use the top-level requirements to adjust deps.  With pants implicit `thrift` requirement injection - this option is off the table. And NB:
```
$ unzip -qc .pants.d/python-setup/resolved_requirements/CPython-2.7.10/twitter.common.recordio-0.3.3-py2-none-any.whl twitter.common.recordio-0.3.3.dist-info/metadata.json | python -mjson.tool | grep -A8 run_requires
    "run_requires": [
        {
            "requires": [
                "thrift (==0.9.1)",
                "twitter.common.log (==0.3.3)"
            ]
        }
    ],
    "version": "0.3.3",
```

The above is an example from apache aurora where the top-level req. is `thrift` injected by PythonChroot and that picks `thrift 0.9.3`.  The second round of resolution includes the `thrift==0.9.1` requirement from `twitter.common.recordio==0.3.3` and this leads to failure since `0.9.3` does not satisfy `==0.9.1`.

At a high level, the 2 thrift requirements considered post-facto should lead to a solution, `thrift,thrift==0.9.1` -> `thrift==0.9.1`.  The issue as explained above is the requirements are resolved in layers with no compatibility smarts at the pex layer and no backtracking to try alternate solutions from prior layers with the current layer.  There is a [TODO in the pex resolver code](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/v1.1.0/pex/resolver.py#L194) to consider backtracking, but this is both of questionable utility (it would be different behavior from pip - which is the behavior folks expect) and a not insignificant change.  As a result pants should probably just provide better control of the injected thrift requirement, possibly using semver constraints as the basis for the default instead of a wide-open dep.  The thrift utility it uses knows the thrift compiler version number after all.

issue_body:
It will be fairly common for a previously accessible Kolibri instance to become unavailable.

Some examples:

* a student may be using a tablet and walk out of range of a hotspot
* a laptop hosting kolibri may run out of battery power
* the host machine remains accessible, but someone shuts down the kolibri instance running on it

We should look at both the technical and UX side of things.

1. Write code to catch and handle errors these kinds of errors holistically, and set some global vuex state that represents whether the host is accessible
2. Add a pop-up, overlay, or similar to indicate to the user that the app is disconnected. Potentially prevent further interaction with the app until it reconnects.  (cc @jtamiace @khangmach)

We probably don't want to go down the 'leave parts usable when disconnected' rabbit hole yet...

issue_title: recommendation: make __init__.py's version a 3-tuple
issue_labels: [{'id': 492822760, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20backend', 'name': 'DEV: backend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 724996906, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20dev%20experience', 'name': 'DEV: dev experience', 'color': 'dfdfdf', 'default': False}, {'id': 732327197, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20distributions', 'name': 'DEV: distributions', 'color': 'dfdfdf', 'default': False}, {'id': 492816641, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TASK:%20tech%20update', 'name': 'TASK: tech update', 'color': '72486f', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

Currently, `__init__.py` looks like this:

```python
#: This string does not reflect alpha/beta/final status and is modified by get_version()
#: Use ``kolibri.__version__`` for the exact version string.
VERSION = (0, 8, 0, 'alpha', 0)
```

The fact that the fourth value (`'alpha'`/`'beta'`/`'final'`) needs to be updated makes creating releases and managing release branches very frustrating:

* we need to do a whole PR+review+merge to set it to `'final'`, create a release tag, then do _another_ PR+review+merge to set it back to either `'alpha'` or `'beta'`
* there doesn't seem to be any difference between `'beta'` and `'alpha'`
* this creates repeated merge conflicts when syncing old releases back to `develop`

### Expected behavior

Since the alpha/beta/final status is reflected in git tags and not in source code, we shouldn't need to update these values in source. Instead, we can simply do:

```python
#: This may not be the exact version as it's subject to modification with
#: get_version() - use ``kolibri.__version__`` for the exact version string.
VERSION = (0, 8, 0)
```

Then the final/beta/alpha status can be driven by git tags and the process for releasing will be greatly simplified

### User-facing consequences

No user-facing consequences, but this would improve developer experience for creating new releases and managing release branches.

### Steps to reproduce

Go through the process of creating a patch release and then syncing those changes back to `develop`

issue_title: Shutdown the ipfs daemon more gracefully
issue_body:
When stopping the ipfs daemon, ipwb currently (https://github.com/oduwsdl/ipwb/blob/master/ipwb/replay.py#L107) runs a `killall ipfs` command via Python subprocess. go-ipfs introduced an `ipfs shutdown` command in version 0.4.10 per https://ipfs.io/blog/27-go-ipfs-0-4-10/

Leverage this if the version of the daemon ≥ 0.4.10. Checking the daemon version will be critical here, as the killall command may still needing in go-ipfs versions < version 0.4.10. This can be accomplished by performing `ipfs shutdown` in a try then forcibly perform the killall if it fails.

issue_title: empty pipe on subprocess restart
issue_body:
<!---
Hello,

We are sorry that you are experiencing an issue with ExaBGP.

Before opening this issue could you please:
 - make sure the problem was not already reported to avoid duplicates.
 - check if the problem is still present on the latest version
   using the 3.4 or master branch (if you know what it means)
   alternatively the latest release (3.4 or 4.0)

For us to help you the outpout of exabgp -d is often required.
Please use ``` for any copied output.
-->

##### Summary

Some explaination is available here: https://gitter.im/Exa-Networks/exabgp?at=5ae9fb0cb37eab7d0460f616

> @thomas-mangin actually, I have some racy condition where it may help....
basically, the subprocess dies (for whatever reason) while exabgp was still answering to a command with a lot of content (say "show adj-rib out extensive" with many routes). Upon restart of the subprocess, the stdin pipe is going to be filled/filling up with the answers from the previous command. Given that the rest of the program assumes an interaction of sending/recving command behind a lock, so everything being sequential, this messes everything up because essentially the first command would read the remaining content from the command before dying until it reads done/error, the second command would read the reply of the first command, the 3rd of the 2nd ...
one of the idea is to bootstrap the subprocess with a "version" command and read from the pipe until we see somehting that starts with "exabgp "

##### OS

```
Linux CentOS 7

```



##### Version

```
ExaBGP : 4.0.2-1c737d99
Python : 2.7.11
Uname  : 4.6
```



##### Steps to reproduce

* exabgp spin up subprocess
* subprocess triggers a command with a lot of outputs (such as "show adj-rib out extensive" with a lot of routes)
* subprocess dies just after sending the command and before finishing reading ALL the content in the pipe
* exabgp restarts the subprocess
* exabgp may still send data to the pipe from the previous command
* the newly restarted subprocess will get the content from the old command the next time it reads from the pipe. Say the command "version" is sent, the next time the subprocess access the pipe, it will get the old content form "show adj-rib out extensive" which has not been read yet.

One way to solve this is to empty the pipe upon starting up. This involved playing with select and non-blocking file as well as potentially allowing some seconds to pass before we can be sure that exabgp will not write more content to the pipe from older commands.

issue_title: Investigate Start processes / Drop privileges order
issue_body:
Hi @thomas-mangin, as discussed in gitter, creating this issue to investigate the possibility of starting subprocesses before dropping the privileges of the ExaBGP process itself.

At the moment, privileges are dropped as ExaBGP daemonizes, which means ExaBGP must run as root if any subprocesses also need to run as root to perform certain actions - of which a good example is `application/healthcheck.py` which attempts to add loopback IP addresses when it starts up and then drops its own privileges.

It would be good, if possible, to allow subprocesses to run with root privileges, and expect these to drop privileges accordingly once they've done any required setup (for example binding to other sub-1024 ports or adding / removing IPs).

issue_title: Request to replace boost Gregorian with Standard C++ component
issue_body:
Currently, due to the use of `boost::gregorian::date`, dates which come from the RDBMS with years outside the range
1400 to 10000
will fail to convert

Replacing `boost::gregorian::date` with standard C++ library components should allow dates outside of this range, and move part of the way to reducing turbodbc dependency on boost.

issue_body:
### What is wrong?

There is (probably) a lot of duplicated logic across services, which is why `p2p.BaseService` was written.

### How can it be fixed

Consider whether anything in `trinity.rpc.ipc` would benefit from using `BaseService`, especially `IPCServer`.

issue_title: Remove `process_type` utility function in favor of `grammar.parse`
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

The `process_type` utility function (found [here](https://github.com/ethereum/eth-abi/blob/master/eth_abi/utils/parsing.py#L11)) is left over from the previous style of parsing type strings.  The `parse` function in the `grammar` module (found [here](https://github.com/ethereum/eth-abi/blob/master/eth_abi/grammar.py#L357)) provides the same functionality and is more extensible.

### How can it be fixed?

We should remove the `process_type` function in favor of the `grammar.parse` function.

issue_title: Re-add `Xmrs.nodeids()` method
issue_body:
This method was dropped in f80ff75b9301f211427b978c1d93d7f066dee1f0, but it was useful. Currently one can only get the list of nodeids from the hidden `_nodeids` list (and modifying that list would cause problems.). Therefore I propose adding support for:

* `Xmrs.nodeids(ivs=None, quantifier=None)`
  Return the list of nodeids for the given intrinsic/bound variables. If `ivs` is `None`, use all nodeids. If `quantifier` is `True`, only return nodeids where each variable in `ivs` is a bound variable (i.e. its EP is a quantifier); if `False`, only return non-quantifiers; if `None`, return both. If a variable in `ivs` is not the intrinsic/bound variable of any EP, raise a `KeyError` (does not raise a `KeyError` if, e.g., the variable exists but is not a bound variable of any EP and `quantifier` is set to `True`)

Also, following the precedent by other methods (e.g., `eps()`, `ep()`), provide the following:

* `Xmrs.nodeid(iv, quantifier=False)`
  Return a single nodeid for the EP whose intrinsic variable is `iv` (not bound variable, because `quantifier` defaults to `False`). Setting `quantifier` to `True` returns only the quantifier with the bound variable `iv`.

issue_title: Don't think features are working properly for nomkl and packages from other channels
issue_body:
I don't have time to fully investigate right now, but I think you get surprisingly different results from:

```
conda create -n nomkl_cf -c conda-forge nomkl numpy scipy pandas jupyter
```

vs.

```
conda create -n nomkl_defaults nomkl numpy scipy pandas jupyter
```

My expectation is that I'd get the nomkl versions in both cases.  Maybe conda-forge numpy **is** a nomkl version, I'm just realizing.  Which then makes me think that maybe we need to reverse the feature logic and have our no-feature numpy look like conda-forge numpy (that seems reasonable to me), in which case our MKL version of numpy should have the [mkl] feature label...

issue_title: Time complexity of AlignmentFile class members references and lengths
issue_body:
Recently I noticed very slow parsing of a BAM file with 500k references, which I thought pretty mysterious. 

The loop iterated over, in part, one of either `AlignmentFile.references` and `AlignmentFile.lengths`. The other was being dereferenced inside the loop.

Eg.
```
for n, reflen in enumerate(bam.lengths):
    refname = bam.references[n]
   ...
```
As these appear as arrays in pysam documentation, I thought this would be O(1) but it clearly was not. Inspecting , libcalignmentfile.pyx I guess the answer is that tuples are built _every call_!

Could the documentation be amended or better yet can memoization be applied to the Cython methods?

issue_title: Use of properties without caching in VariantRecord could cause unintentional performance problems
[Enhancement] Add option to abort the compile
issue_body:
Sometimes the compilation may need too much time (especially if we add additional pre-parsers) or there is something borked. Then "abort compile" would be very helpfull as the current implementation just disables compile/run in this case.

This would be the things to implement

* add an abort function and button
* if compile or run is used enable the abort button
* when clicked: abort started processes (if possible)
* delete intermediate files (if generated/copied by OCIDE and not locked)
* enable compile/run, disable abort

issue_title: run_transaction and session.close()
issue_body:
We've been having some issues with the connection pool of SQLalchemy becoming stale and have explicitly defined `session.close()` (via context manager) on our non-transactional reads.

As `run_transcation` when used with a sessionmaker generates the session internally we are wanting to know if there are any known issues resulting in failure to return connections to the pool.

We're just postgres currently, but using cockroachdb binding for this to allow easier migration in future.

issue_title: Inconsistent compatibility versioning + outdated README
issue_body:
The README needs to be updated, because it currently states the following version compatibility:

```
# Elasticsearch 1.0
elasticsearch>=1.0.0,<2.0.0
```

But that is not the case.  I recently installed 1.7.0 and it broke our calls to `put_mapping` because `ignore_conflicts` was removed as a parameter for 2.0 compatibility in commit 36d8d4b545938fd37988b72ccb8e19b58fe62882.

Perhaps the ES2.0 compatible versions of elasticsearch-py should start with v2.0.0, and leave <2.0.0 for ES1.0?  Ideally it'd look something like this:

```
# Elasticsearch 2.0
elasticsearch>=2.0.0,<3.0.0

# Elasticsearch 1.0
elasticsearch>=1.0.0,<2.0.0
```

issue_title: is extension supposed to only work once?
issue_body:
I see similar behavior with Windows and Linux customscript, looks like the first run result is cached and following run just show first run's result.

for example, I have a json file  
cat script-centos.json
{
  "fileUris": ["https://mystorageshare.blob.core.windows.net/publicshare/centos.sh"],
  "commandToExecute": "sh  ./centos.sh"
}
When I run 
az vm extension set -g rg --vm-name centosvm1 --name CustomScriptForLinux --publisher Microsoft.OSTCExtensions --settings script-centos.json --protected-settings "{}"

the first time, I got error "The specified blob does not exist", cause I forgot to upload centos.sh to the storage account. then I uploaded it and rerun the command, still got the same error even when the file is there now.

To make it work, I need to delete the previous extension first:
az vm extension delete -g rg --vm-name centosvm1 --name CustomScriptForLinux

then it will work.

issue_title: KeyVault and MSI is broken in recent CLI releases
issue_body:
More precisely, since IMDS was introduced in msrestazure (March 28, 0.4.25)
Bug in msrestazure:
https://github.com/Azure/msrestazure-for-python/issues/109

This is because `resource` was moved to `_ImdsTokenProvider` internally, and then the `resource` at the credentials level was not used anymore. But KeyVault is using it to change the resource on the fly. This makes all KV call from MSI `Unauthorized` from a VM using IMDS.

Fixed in msrestazure 0.4.35 (unreleased yet).
https://github.com/Azure/msrestazure-for-python/issues/109

Created for tracking, in case someone has the issue and google it here.

FYI @yugangw-msft @williexu @troydai

issue_title: Why is LongRunningOperations so opinionated about response codes from the service?
issue_body:
I was going through the code and saw that the code (for example) only allows for 201 from a PUT operation. I would imagine that a 201 result from a POST would be valid as well, no?

    def _raise_if_bad_http_status_and_method(self, response):
        """Check response status code is valid for a Put or Patch
        request. Must be 200, 201, 202, or 204.
        :raises: BadStatus if invalid status.
        """
        code = response.status_code
        if code in {200, 202} or \
           (code == 201 and self.method == 'PUT') or \
           (code == 204 and self.method in {'DELETE', 'POST'}):
            return
        raise BadStatus(
            "Invalid return status for {!r} operation".format(self.method))
